description: Comprehensive pre-launch PRD: security fixes, data-integrity, functionality, launch-critical features, and core UX improvements. P1=security/data-loss, P2=functionality-breaks, P3=core UX features, P4=deferrable polish.

userStories.title: Sanitize RichTextEditor HTML output to prevent XSS
userStories.description: As a user, I need HTML content stored and rendered by the app to be safe so malicious scripts in script/template bodies cannot execute in the browser.
acceptanceCriteria: DOMPurify (or equivalent) sanitizes HTML in RichTextEditor.tsx before calling onChange
ScriptModal.tsx sanitizes content before Supabase insert
TemplateModal.tsx sanitizes body before Supabase insert
Pasted content with <script> tags is stripped on input
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazard: frontend/src/components/RichTextEditor.tsx — onChange outputs raw HTML, dangerouslySetInnerHTML enables XSS. Also affects ScriptModal.tsx and TemplateModal.tsx.

userStories.title: Add CSRF nonce to all OAuth state params
userStories.description: As a user, I need OAuth flows to be protected against state forgery so an attacker cannot complete OAuth on my behalf.
acceptanceCriteria: GmailOAuthButton.tsx adds lib/oauth-csrf.ts nonce to state param before redirect
GoogleCalendarOAuthButton.tsx adds nonce to state param
SalesforceOAuthButton.tsx adds nonce to state param
OutlookOAuthButton.tsx adds nonce to state param
OutlookCalendarOAuthButton.tsx adds nonce to state param
Each callback page validates nonce via validateOAuthState before proceeding
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazard: GmailOAuthButton.tsx — state param contains user_id without CSRF nonce; GoogleCalendarOAuthButton.tsx, SalesforceOAuthButton.tsx, OutlookOAuthButton.tsx, OutlookCalendarOAuthButton.tsx — same pattern.

userStories.title: Add ProtectedRoute guard to Diagnostics page
userStories.description: As an operator, I need the Diagnostics page to be authentication-gated so unauthenticated users cannot access internal DB state.
acceptanceCriteria: Diagnostics.tsx route in App.tsx is wrapped in ProtectedRoute
Diagnostics page renders a read-only mode by default (SELECT-only queries, no writes)
Admin-write mode requires an explicit ?admin=true query param AND authenticated session
Direct URL access while unauthenticated redirects to /signin
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazard: frontend/src/pages/Diagnostics.tsx — no auth guard, direct URL exposes internal DB state; each test fires real Supabase writes in production.

userStories.title: Fix SignUp to rollback on org/user insert failure
userStories.description: As a new user, I need sign-up to either fully succeed or fully fail so I never end up authenticated with a broken account state.
acceptanceCriteria: SignUp.tsx catches org/user insert failure after successful auth.signUp
On insert failure: calls supabase.auth.signOut() and displays error message
User is not left in authenticated-but-broken state
Invitation_id fetched only when search param present; expired invitations show clear error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazard: frontend/src/pages/SignUp.tsx — if signUp succeeds but org/user insert fails, user lands in authenticated-but-broken state with no recovery path.

userStories.title: Add missing routes for Arena and ContentLibrary pages
userStories.description: As a user, I need Arena and ContentLibrary to be reachable via navigation so pages that exist in the codebase are actually accessible.
acceptanceCriteria: App.tsx includes lazy import for Arena.tsx at /arena
App.tsx includes lazy import for ContentLibrary.tsx at /content-library
Both routes are wrapped in ProtectedRoute
Both routes are added to the AppLayout nav so they appear in sidebar
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazard: frontend/src/App.tsx — Add routes for /arena and /content-library; these pages exist as .tsx files but have no Route entries.

userStories.title: Add root ErrorBoundary, Sentry error tracking, and per-OAuth error boundaries
userStories.description: As a user, I need render errors to show a recoverable error screen rather than a blank white page so I understand what went wrong.
acceptanceCriteria: main.tsx wraps <App /> in a root ErrorBoundary component that renders a user-visible error message
SettingsPage.tsx wraps each of the 5 OAuth integration buttons in its own ErrorBoundary
Single OAuth button throw no longer crashes the entire integrations tab
ErrorBoundary displays fallback UI with a reload CTA
Typecheck passes
Sentry DSN configured via VITE_SENTRY_DSN env var
Unhandled errors captured in Sentry with user context
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazard: frontend/src/main.tsx — no root ErrorBoundary causes blank white screen; frontend/src/pages/SettingsPage.tsx — single OAuth throw crashes entire integrations tab. | FEAT-002 merged here — install @sentry/react in main.tsx alongside ErrorBoundary

userStories.title: Fix AddContactModal to pass org_id from auth context
userStories.description: As a rep, I need contacts I create to always be linked to my org so contacts are not created without org association.
acceptanceCriteria: AddContactModal.tsx reads org_id from useAuth() hook
Contact insert payload includes org_id field
If org_id is null, insert is blocked and user sees an error
Existing contacts with null org_id are NOT modified (migration out of scope)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazard: frontend/src/components/AddContactModal.tsx — contact insert does not link to org_id from session; contacts may be created without org association.

userStories.title: Add ON CONFLICT handling to ImportCSVModal list_contacts insert
userStories.description: As a rep, I need CSV import to handle duplicates gracefully so re-importing a list does not create duplicate junction rows.
acceptanceCriteria: ImportCSVModal.tsx list_contacts batch insert uses ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
Per-row contact upsert is batched (max 500 rows per chunk) not N individual queries
Import summary correctly reports inserted vs skipped counts
Import of 5000+ contacts completes without timeout
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazard: frontend/src/components/ImportCSVModal.tsx — list_contacts batch insert lacks ON CONFLICT; per-row duplicate check is N queries for N rows.

userStories.title: Fix Pipeline drag StrictMode double-fire and add optimistic rollback
userStories.description: As a rep, I need drag-and-drop deal stage updates to be reliable so a failed DB update does not silently desync the board.
acceptanceCriteria: Pipeline.tsx onDragEnd handler is idempotent (no duplicate stage updates in StrictMode)
Failed Supabase stage update reverts deal card to original column
User sees an error toast on stage update failure
No console errors related to @hello-pangea/dnd in StrictMode
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazard: frontend/src/pages/Pipeline.tsx — @hello-pangea/dnd StrictMode double-invocation; no optimistic rollback on failed stage update.

userStories.title: Add calendar pre-check and failure feedback to BookMeetingModal
userStories.description: As a rep, I need BookMeetingModal to verify a calendar is connected before booking so I always know whether a calendar event was actually created.
acceptanceCriteria: BookMeetingModal.tsx checks getCalendarConnection() before rendering the time slot form
If no calendar connected, renders CTA to connect calendar in Settings (not blank form)
If createCalendarEvent fails after form submit, user sees explicit error (not silent success)
Activity log entry is still created even if calendar event creation fails
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazard: frontend/src/components/BookMeetingModal.tsx — createCalendarEvent silently fails if no calendar connected; user thinks meeting booked but no event exists.

userStories.title: Fix ContactDetailPage null back navigation
userStories.description: As a rep, I need the back button on a contact detail page to always navigate to a valid route so direct URL navigation does not break the back button.
acceptanceCriteria: ContactDetailPage.tsx back navigation uses location.state?.from ?? '/lists' as fallback
Direct URL navigation to /contacts/:id no longer breaks the back button
Inline field save failure shows error toast (not silent failure)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazard: frontend/src/pages/ContactDetailPage.tsx — useLocation back navigation depends on location.state.from; if navigated directly, state is null.

userStories.title: Add idempotency guards to Stripe webhook edge function
userStories.description: As an operator, I need Stripe webhook processing to be idempotent so duplicate webhook deliveries do not create duplicate subscription state.
acceptanceCriteria: handle-stripe-webhook/index.ts tracks processed event IDs in a Supabase table or Stripe idempotency key
Duplicate event_id returns 200 without reprocessing
Missing org_id or user_id in metadata returns 400 with log entry (not silent failure)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazard: supabase/functions/handle-stripe-webhook/index.ts — no idempotency guard; duplicate webhook fires create duplicate state updates; missing org_id silently breaks.

userStories.title: Add idempotency guards to Salesforce sync and email reply tracker
userStories.description: As an operator, I need activity sync jobs to be idempotent so duplicate webhook fires do not create duplicate Salesforce Tasks or reply records.
acceptanceCriteria: sync-activities-to-salesforce/index.ts adds idempotency guard (check existing Task ID before insert)
track-email-replies/index.ts adds idempotency guard (skip activity if replied_at already set)
One contact failure in Salesforce sync does not retry entire batch
Token expiration check added before Salesforce API calls
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazards: sync-activities-to-salesforce — no idempotency, no partial failure recovery, token not checked; track-email-replies — same patterns.

userStories.title: Add team invitation transactional rollback on email failure
userStories.description: As a manager, I need team invitation to either send successfully or clean up its database row so failed invitations can be resent.
acceptanceCriteria: send-team-invitation/index.ts deletes the invitation row if Resend/SMTP email send fails
Frontend caller receives clear error and can retry
Invitation expiry is enforced server-side at acceptance time (e.g., 7 days)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazard: supabase/functions/send-team-invitation/index.ts — email failure leaves invitation row in pending state with no email sent; stale links remain valid indefinitely.

userStories.title: Add concurrent refresh lock for Google and Microsoft token refresh
userStories.description: As a user, I need token refresh to handle concurrent calls safely so two in-flight refresh requests do not overwrite each other's tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: refresh-google-token/index.ts adds optimistic lock (check token valid before calling Google endpoint)
refresh-microsoft-token/index.ts mirrors same lock pattern
invalid_grant from either provider marks oauth_connections row as disconnected
Frontend token-refresh.ts adds one retry on transient network failure before returning null
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazards: refresh-google-token — no lock on concurrent refresh calls; refresh-microsoft-token — same; token-refresh.ts — no retry on transient failures. | Already implemented: refreshInFlight Map in token-refresh.ts lines 19-62

userStories.title: Add Salesforce connection pre-check to activity log modals and SOQL guard
userStories.description: As a rep, I need Salesforce sync in activity modals to fail gracefully so a disconnected integration does not silently error on every activity log.
acceptanceCriteria: LogActivityModal.tsx calls getSalesforceConnection() before markActivityForSync; skips sync and shows warning if not connected
LogSocialActivityModal.tsx applies same pre-check
salesforce.ts objectType parameter is validated against an allowlist before use in SOQL query
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazards: LogActivityModal, LogSocialActivityModal — markActivityForSync without connection check; salesforce.ts — SOQL injection on unguarded objectType.

userStories.title: Add delete confirmations for SalesBlocks, Scripts, Goals, and EmailTemplates
userStories.description: As a rep, I need destructive delete actions to require confirmation so accidental clicks do not permanently destroy sessions or content.
acceptanceCriteria: SalesBlocks.tsx shows confirmation modal before deleting a SalesBlock
Scripts.tsx shows confirmation dialog before deleting a script
Goals.tsx shows confirmation dialog before deleting a goal
EmailTemplates.tsx shows confirmation dialog before deleting a template
ListDetailPage.tsx replaces browser confirm() with modal matching bulk delete pattern
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazards: SalesBlocks.tsx — delete fires immediately; Scripts.tsx — no confirmation; Goals.tsx — single click permanently removes goal; ListDetailPage.tsx — uses browser confirm().

userStories.title: Fix useTheme listener cleanup and write failure feedback
userStories.description: As a user, I need theme preference to persist correctly so a failed Supabase write is visible and stale event listeners don't accumulate.
acceptanceCriteria: useTheme.ts useEffect returns cleanup that removes the matchMedia change listener
If Supabase write fails, theme preference reverts to previous value (not silent divergence)
User sees a toast warning if theme preference could not be saved
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazards: frontend/src/hooks/useTheme.ts — system theme listener not cleaned up on unmount; write fails silently while UI shows new theme.

userStories.title: Fix Arena void-700 missing Tailwind colour class
userStories.description: As a user, I need the Arena leaderboard to render correctly so rank colours display properly without falling back to undefined CSS.
acceptanceCriteria: tailwind.config.js includes void-700 in the theme color palette
Arena.tsx rank colour renders the correct VV design token colour
No Tailwind undefined class warnings in browser console
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazard: frontend/src/pages/Arena.tsx — void-700 missing from tailwind.config.js; rank colour fallback uses undefined colour class.

userStories.title: Fix ProtectedRoute redirect loop and useAuth loading race
userStories.description: As a user, I need auth routing to be stable so concurrent logout does not cause a redirect loop and loading states do not leave me on a blank screen.
acceptanceCriteria: ProtectedRoute.tsx does not redirect to /signin until loading=false AND user=null (both conditions)
Concurrent logout does not trigger multiple /signin redirects
useAuth.ts loading flag remains true until first getSession resolves
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazards: ProtectedRoute.tsx — synchronous redirect causes loop on concurrent logout; useAuth.ts — loading=false but user still null race condition.

userStories.title: Fix TrialExpiryBanner dismiss persistence
userStories.description: As a user, I need the trial expiry banner dismiss to persist across navigation so I am not shown the same banner on every page change.
acceptanceCriteria: TrialExpiryBanner.tsx persists dismissed=true to localStorage keyed by orgId + trial_ends_at
Dismissed banner does not re-appear on route change
Banner re-appears after 24 hours (localStorage TTL)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazard: frontend/src/components/TrialExpiryBanner.tsx — dismissal in React local state only; reappears on navigation.

userStories.title: Add time-range filter to Analytics page
userStories.description: As a manager, I need Analytics to support time-range filtering so activity count queries do not grow unboundedly as the database grows.
acceptanceCriteria: Analytics.tsx has a time-range selector (Last 7 days / 30 days / 90 days / All time)
All activity aggregation queries include created_at filter matching selected range
Default range is Last 30 days
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazard: frontend/src/pages/Analytics.tsx — no time-range filter on activity aggregation; loads all-time counts growing unbounded.

userStories.title: Add pagination to ContactActivityTimeline and Email page
userStories.description: As a rep, I need activity feeds to be paginated so high-volume contacts and email lists do not cause slow queries and blank-state delays.
acceptanceCriteria: ContactActivityTimeline.tsx fetches first 20 activities; Load more button fetches next 20
Email.tsx paginates email activity list (25 items per page or infinite scroll)
Both components handle empty states with clear messaging
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazards: ContactActivityTimeline.tsx — 100+ activities cause unbounded query; Email.tsx — no pagination, large volumes cause slow query.

userStories.title: Fix Home.tsx CreateSalesBlockModal post-create callback
userStories.description: As a rep, I need a newly created SalesBlock to appear on the Home dashboard immediately so I don't need to reload the page after creating one.
acceptanceCriteria: Home.tsx passes a callback to CreateSalesBlockModal that re-fetches the salesblocks query on close
New SalesBlock card appears in dashboard without page reload
React Query invalidateQueries or refetch called correctly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazard: frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx — CreateSalesBlockModal launched from Home must re-fetch salesblocks after close; if callback not wired, new block won't appear.

userStories.title: Fix ForgotPassword SITE_URL to use correct production origin
userStories.description: As a user trying to reset my password, I need reset links to point to the correct production URL so reset emails do not contain localhost links.
acceptanceCriteria: ForgotPassword.tsx uses VITE_SITE_URL env var as redirectTo (not window.location.origin)
VITE_SITE_URL is set in production Vercel env vars to the production domain
Dev environment correctly uses localhost from .env.local
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb hazard: frontend/src/pages/ForgotPassword.tsx — window.location.origin used for redirectTo; in dev points to localhost; reset links expire if opened in different environment.

userStories.title: Add Google SSO and returnTo deep link on SignIn page
userStories.description: As a user, I need to sign in with Google and have my destination preserved so I can reach deep-linked pages directly after authentication.
acceptanceCriteria: SignIn.tsx has a 'Continue with Google' button that triggers Supabase social login
SignIn.tsx reads a returnTo query param and redirects to that path after successful sign-in
returnTo value is validated to be a relative path (prevents open redirect)
Rate limit error from Supabase shows lockout feedback message
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/pages/SignIn.tsx — Add returnTo param to preserve destination URL. Add Google SSO as alternative. Add lockout feedback for rate limit errors. [MOVED from roadmap — pre-launch blocker]

userStories.title: Pull pricing from Supabase config table instead of hardcoded constants
userStories.description: As a product manager, I need pricing to be stored in Supabase so price changes do not require a code deploy and Stripe price IDs stay in sync.
acceptanceCriteria: Supabase table pricing_plans stores: id, name, monthly_price_cents, annual_price_cents, stripe_price_id_monthly, stripe_price_id_annual, features (jsonb)
PricingPage.tsx fetches from pricing_plans table on mount
create-checkout-session edge function uses Stripe price ID from pricing_plans (not hardcoded)
PricingPage shows loading skeleton and error toast (not alert) on load failure
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/pages/PricingPage.tsx — Pull pricing from Supabase table to avoid price drift. Add error toast instead of alert. [MOVED from roadmap — pre-launch blocker]

userStories.title: Add invite member flow to Team page
userStories.description: As a manager, I need an invite button on the Team page so I can onboard new reps without leaving the team management view.
acceptanceCriteria: Team.tsx has an Invite Member button that triggers the send-team-invitation flow
Invite modal collects email and role (sdr | ae | manager)
Success state shows confirmation message; failure shows error with retry
Time-range filter on activity aggregation (Last 30 days / 90 days / All time)
Empty team state renders onboarding CTA
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/pages/Team.tsx — Add time-range filter for activity aggregation. Handle empty org_id gracefully. Add invite member flow. [MOVED from roadmap — pre-launch blocker]

userStories.title: Add Analytics time-range filter and Recharts trend visualisation
userStories.description: As a manager, I need to filter analytics by time range and see trend lines so I can assess team performance over specific periods.
acceptanceCriteria: Analytics.tsx has time-range selector: Last 7 / 30 / 90 days
KPI cards update to reflect selected time range
Recharts LineChart renders activity trend per day for calls, emails, and meetings
KPI card loading skeleton shown during data fetch
Custom KPI target input validates min=0, max=10000 with inline error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/pages/Analytics.tsx — Add time-range filter. Add loading skeleton per KPI card. Validate custom KPI bounds. Consider Recharts for trend visualisation. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Add Arena real-time leaderboard and time-range filter
userStories.description: As a rep, I need the leaderboard to update in real-time and support time-range filtering so competitions are current and fairly measured.
acceptanceCriteria: Arena.tsx subscribes to Supabase real-time on the activities table for live score updates
Time-range filter: Today / This Week / This Month / All Time
Leaderboard is scoped to org_id (no cross-org data)
Real-time subscription cleaned up on component unmount
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/pages/Arena.tsx — Consider real-time subscription for live leaderboard. Add time-range filter. Enforce org-scoped leaderboard. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Add Pipeline deal value totals and empty-state improvements
userStories.description: As a rep, I need to see total deal value per pipeline stage and have meaningful empty states so I can assess pipeline value at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Pipeline.tsx column header shows total deal value (sum) for all deals in that stage
Empty pipeline shows a Create Your First Deal CTA
Failed stage update shows error toast and reverts card to original column
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/pages/Pipeline.tsx — Add deal value total in column header. Add optimistic rollback for failed stage updates. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Add Goals timezone-correct aggregation and empty state
userStories.description: As a rep, I need goal progress to calculate correctly at day/week boundaries and show a helpful empty state when no goals exist.
acceptanceCriteria: Goals.tsx activity aggregation uses UTC-based day boundaries (not browser local time)
Empty goal list shows a Create Your First Goal CTA
Delete goal action shows a confirmation modal before permanent removal
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/pages/Goals.tsx — Fix time-zone boundary for daily/weekly aggregation. Add empty state. Add delete confirmation. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Add SalesBlocks status filter tabs and empty state
userStories.description: As a rep, I need to filter SalesBlocks by status and see an empty state so managing active vs completed sessions is clear.
acceptanceCriteria: SalesBlocks.tsx has filter tabs: All / Active / Completed / Scheduled
Empty state shows a Create SalesBlock CTA with description
Delete SalesBlock shows confirmation modal (from FIX-017)
salesblocks query verified to be scoped by org_id
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/pages/SalesBlocks.tsx — Add delete confirmation dialog. Audit salesblocks query for org_id scoping. Add empty state. Consider status filter tabs. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Add Scripts search, categories, and SalesBlock linking
userStories.description: As a rep, I need to search scripts and tag them by category so finding the right script during a call is fast and contextual.
acceptanceCriteria: Scripts.tsx has a search input filtering by title/content
Script cards have a category/tag field (configurable freeform text)
ScriptModal.tsx collects category on create/edit
Scripts page allows filtering by category
Optional: link a script to a SalesBlock so it auto-loads during a session
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/pages/Scripts.tsx — Add script categories/tags. Add search. Link scripts to SalesBlock session. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Add EmailTemplates preview and search/filter
userStories.description: As a rep, I need to preview email templates and search across them so selecting the right template when composing is quick.
acceptanceCriteria: EmailTemplates.tsx has a search input filtering by subject/name
Template card has an expand/preview button showing rendered HTML body
Delete failure shows error toast (not silent failure)
Verified RLS scopes email_templates to org_id
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/pages/EmailTemplates.tsx — Add error toast on delete failure. Verify RLS. Add template preview and search/filter. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Add template insertion to ComposeEmailModal
userStories.description: As a rep, I need to insert an email template into the compose modal so I can send consistent, pre-approved messaging without copying manually.
acceptanceCriteria: ComposeEmailModal.tsx has an Insert Template button that opens a template picker
Template picker shows filterable list from email_templates table
Selected template populates subject and body fields
Existing body content is replaced with confirmation prompt before overwrite
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/components/ComposeEmailModal.tsx — Add template insertion from EmailTemplates. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Consolidate Microsoft OAuth buttons into single MicrosoftOAuthButton
userStories.description: As a developer, I need OutlookOAuthButton and OutlookCalendarOAuthButton consolidated so fixes only need to be made in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: New component MicrosoftOAuthButton.tsx accepts an integrationType prop: 'mail' | 'calendar'
integrationType='mail' uses Mail.Send + Mail.ReadBasic scopes
integrationType='calendar' uses Calendar.ReadWrite scopes
OutlookOAuthButton.tsx and OutlookCalendarOAuthButton.tsx replaced with MicrosoftOAuthButton usage
CSRF nonce added to state param (FIX-002 applied here)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/components/OutlookOAuthButton.tsx — Consolidate OutlookOAuthButton and OutlookCalendarOAuthButton into a single MicrosoftOAuthButton parameterized by integration type. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Consolidate Gmail and Google Calendar OAuth buttons into shared hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Google OAuth button logic extracted into a shared hook so Gmail and Calendar flows share one implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: New hook useGoogleOAuth.ts accepts a provider param: 'gmail' | 'google_calendar'
Hook returns: initiateOAuth, connectionStatus, disconnect
GmailOAuthButton.tsx and GoogleCalendarOAuthButton.tsx use useGoogleOAuth hook
Scope sets correctly differentiate: gmail.readonly for Gmail, calendar.events for Calendar
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/components/GoogleCalendarOAuthButton.tsx — Consolidate OAuth button logic with GmailOAuthButton into a shared hook with provider param. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Add SettingsPage per-OAuth ErrorBoundaries and success/error toasts
userStories.description: As a user, I need settings changes to provide clear success/failure feedback and OAuth errors to be contained so one broken integration doesn't crash the whole page.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of the 5 OAuth integration buttons is wrapped in its own ErrorBoundary
Profile save, org save, pipeline stage save all show success toast on completion
All settings save failures show error toast (not silent failure)
Each settings tab extracted into its own sub-component
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/pages/SettingsPage.tsx — Wrap each OAuth button in ErrorBoundary. Add success/error toasts for all save actions. Extract each tab into a sub-component. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Add configurable activity types via org settings
userStories.description: As a manager, I need activity types to be configurable per org so teams can add custom call outcomes without requiring a code deploy.
acceptanceCriteria: Supabase table activity_types stores: id, org_id, label, value, icon
LogActivityModal.tsx fetches activity_types for the org on mount
Default types (call, email, meeting, note, social) are seeded on org creation
Managers can add/remove types in SettingsPage
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/components/LogActivityModal.tsx — Make activity types configurable via org settings. Add outcome field for calls. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Add batch list_contacts count query to fix Lists N+1 pattern
userStories.description: As a rep, I need the Lists page to load quickly so contact counts don't trigger N separate queries for N lists.
acceptanceCriteria: Lists.tsx replaces per-list count query with a single GROUP BY aggregation query
Contact counts still display correctly per list card
getSalesforceConnection errors caught and handled (does not crash Lists page)
Lists page load time does not scale linearly with list count
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/pages/Lists.tsx — Batch list_contacts count into single query. Add error catch around getSalesforceConnection. Add list sorting and search. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Add ContactActivityTimeline cursor-based pagination
userStories.description: As a rep, I need the contact activity timeline to load incrementally so high-volume contacts don't cause unbounded queries and slow renders.
acceptanceCriteria: ContactActivityTimeline.tsx fetches first 20 activities ordered by created_at DESC
Load More button fetches next 20 using cursor (last activity ID)
Activity type icon map has a fallback icon for unmapped types
userId prop passed to filter own activities where relevant
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/components/ContactActivityTimeline.tsx — Add pagination or cursor-based infinite scroll. Expand activity type icon map with fallback icon. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Add ContactDetailPage 404 state and server-side sort for large lists
userStories.description: As a rep, I need a proper 404 screen when a contact doesn't exist and for lists to sort server-side so large contact volumes perform correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: ContactDetailPage.tsx renders a 404 Not Found UI when contactId returns no data
ListDetailPage.tsx sort state passes ORDER BY to Supabase query (not client-side)
Back navigation null guard applied: location.state?.from ?? '/lists'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompts: ContactDetailPage — Add 404 state when contact not found. ListDetailPage — Move sort to server-side when list grows. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Add Social page empty state and date range filter
userStories.description: As a rep, I need the Social page to show a helpful empty state and allow date filtering so I can review social activity for specific periods.
acceptanceCriteria: Social.tsx shows an empty state with a Log Activity CTA when no social activities exist
Null contact joins (deleted contact) render contact name as 'Deleted Contact' not blank/error
Date range filter: Last 7 / 30 / 90 days
platform field presence verified on activities table before filtering
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/pages/Social.tsx — Add null guard for deleted contacts. Add empty state with CTA. Add date range filter. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Add merge tag validation and preview to TemplateModal
userStories.description: As a rep, I need template merge tags validated at save time and previewed at send time so raw {{first_name}} tags are never sent to prospects.
acceptanceCriteria: TemplateModal.tsx validates {{merge_tags}} at save time against a known variable list
Unknown merge tags produce a warning (not blocking error) at save
Send-time preview in ComposeEmailModal substitutes merge tags with contact values
Subject line capped at 200 characters; body capped at 50,000 characters
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/components/TemplateModal.tsx — Validate merge tags at save time. Add send-time preview with variable substitution. Enforce limits. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Add Home dashboard skeleton loaders and empty-state messaging
userStories.description: As a rep, I need the Home dashboard to show skeleton placeholders while loading and meaningful empty states when no data exists.
acceptanceCriteria: Home.tsx shows skeleton loader per data section independently (not full-page spinner)
Goals section shows 'Set your first goal' CTA when goals array is empty
SalesBlock schedule shows 'No sessions today' when no blocks are scheduled
Activity feed shows 'No recent activity' when activities array is empty
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx — Add empty-state messaging when goals have no activities. Consider skeleton loaders per data section independently. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Add ImportCSVModal batch insert and streaming progress updates
userStories.description: As a rep, I need CSV import to handle large files efficiently and show real-time progress so I know how the import is going for 5000+ contacts.
acceptanceCriteria: ImportCSVModal.tsx inserts contacts in chunks of 500 rows per batch
Progress bar updates after each batch completes (X of N contacts imported)
Import summary shows inserted, updated, skipped, and failed counts
List assignment uses ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING (from FIX-008)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/components/ImportCSVModal.tsx — Add batch insert for contacts (chunk 500). Consider streaming progress updates during import. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Add ContentLibrary redundancy review and merge/split decision
userStories.description: As a product manager, I need ContentLibrary, EmailTemplates, and Scripts pages to be clearly differentiated so users aren't confused by overlapping content views.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit: identify whether ContentLibrary adds distinct value over EmailTemplates + Scripts pages
If redundant: ContentLibrary redirects to a unified /library view replacing both separate pages
If distinct: ContentLibrary has clear description text explaining its purpose vs scripts/templates
RLS on email_templates and call_scripts verified to scope to same org_id
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: frontend/src/pages/ContentLibrary.tsx — Merge with EmailTemplates and Scripts pages or remove redundancy. Verify org-scoped RLS. [UI feature — core UX work]

userStories.title: Add team invitation resend endpoint and expiry enforcement
userStories.description: As a manager, I need to resend team invitations and have expired links rejected so the invitation flow is reliable and secure.
acceptanceCriteria: send-team-invitation edge function supports a resend=true mode that deletes previous pending row and creates new invitation
Invitation expiry enforced server-side at acceptance time (reject if created_at > 7 days ago)
SettingsPage Invite tab shows pending invitations with Resend action
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Breadcrumb prompt: supabase/functions/send-team-invitation/index.ts — Add transactional rollback if email send fails. Add resend endpoint. Enforce invitation expiry at acceptance time. [UI feature — core UX work]
